Code fragment: condition = NOTICE.TITLE.containsIgnoreCase(keyword);
Explanation: Using `containsIgnoreCase` performs a full-text search with LIKE '%keyword%', which cannot use indexes and causes full table scans. Consider using a dedicated full-text search engine like Elasticsearch or database-native full-text search capabilities.

Code fragment: condition = NOTICE.CONTENTS.containsIgnoreCase(keyword);
Explanation: Using `containsIgnoreCase` on the CONTENTS column performs an inefficient LIKE '%keyword%' search that scans the entire table. Consider implementing full-text search using database-native features (e.g., PostgreSQL's tsvector) or a search engine like Elasticsearch.

Code fragment:
condition = NOTICE.TITLE.containsIgnoreCase(keyword)
.or(NOTICE.CONTENTS.containsIgnoreCase(keyword));
